title: Aphyllon vallicola
intro: Aphyllon vallicola, also known as Aphyllon fasciculatum, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including pain and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Aphyllon vallicola include alkaloids, particularly the glycoside aphyllonine, as well as other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aphyllon vallicola is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, often used to treat conditions such as fever and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Aphyllon vallicola is a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to North America. This plant is also known as the vallicola or sand dodder. Aphyllon vallicola is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants. The classification of Aphyllon vallicola is further categorized as a member of the genus Aphyllon.
